data.title: Stack Capital Group announces best efforts financing and concurrent private placement for up to $25M
data.description:
• Stack Capital Group (TSX:STCK:CA) on Thursday announced the terms of a "best efforts" private placement and concurrent non-brokered private placement for aggregate gross proceeds to the company of up to $25M.
  • In connection with the offering, the company announced an agreement in connection with a "best efforts" private placement of up to 727,272 units at a price of $13.75 per unit for gross proceeds of up to $10M (the "LIFE Offering").
  • In addition, the company also announces the terms of a concurrent non-brokered private placement of up to 1.09M units at the offering price to certain investors, for gross proceeds of up to $15M or such higher number as determined by the company in its discretion.
  • The net proceeds of the offering will be used for investments and general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about August 8.
  • At the closing of the offering, the company will pay to the agents a cash fee equal to 5.0% of the gross proceeds raised in connection with the offering.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Spruce Biosciences to enact 1-for-75 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Spruce Biosciences (OTC:SPRB) will implement a 1-for-75 reverse stock split effective August 4, 2025.

  • Post-split trading will begin on August 5, 2025, under the same ticker “SPRB”.

  • The reverse split aims to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1 minimum bid requirement.

  • Outstanding shares will shrink from ~42.2M to ~0.6M.

  • Source: Press release

data.title: Gouverneur Bancorp approves second stock repurchase program
data.description:
• Gouverneur Bancorp (OTCQB:GOVB) on Thursday announced a new stock repurchase program to repurchase up to 52,778 shares, or 5%, of the company’s outstanding common stock.
  • The company’s new stock repurchase program will terminate upon the completion of the purchase of 52,778 shares or on July 24, 2026 if not all shares have been purchased by that date.
  • On December 11, 2024, the company announced its first stock repurchase program, which authorized the purchase of up to 55,356 shares.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SkyWest GAAP EPS of $2.91 beats by $0.63, revenue of $1.04B beats by $60.62M
data.description:
• SkyWest press release (NASDAQ:SKYW): Q2 GAAP EPS of $2.91 beats by $0.63.
  • Revenue of $1.04B (+19.9% Y/Y) beats by $60.62M.
  • Q2 2025 block hour production increased by 19% compared to Q2 2024 and 7% compared to Q1 2025

data.title: Wall Street ended mixed after Tesla and Alphabet earnings and rising yields
data.description:
Wall Street expressed a cautious tone on Thursday, with the major averages closing mixed as investors weighed a wave of corporate earnings—highlighted by results from Alphabet and Tesla—against rising Treasury yields following an unexpected fall in jobless claims.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was the weakest as the index ended lower by 0.7%. At the same time the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) rounded out the session near flat and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) concluded higher by 0.1%.

Yields pushed up with the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rising by 2 basis points to 4.40%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved higher by 3 basis points to 3.92%.

The move up in yields comes as the latest jobless claims data fell, signaling continued resilience in the labor market—potentially giving the Federal Reserve more justification to maintain its current interest rate stance. 

Initial jobless claims for the week declined by 4K to 217K from 221K in the prior week (unrevised), and lower than the 227K consensus, according to data released by the Department of Labor. 

Amid heightened tensions between President Trump and the Federal Reserve, the president is slated to make a rare visit to the central bank on Thursday—marking the first such trip by a sitting U.S. president in nearly 20 years. The meeting is scheduled for 4 p.m. ET.

Also on the economic front, new home sales rose less than expected in the month of June. 

"The VIX remains tame and steady at moderate levels. This is an indication that investors are increasingly comfortable with the current trading environment, and expect it to continue. It also suggests a high level of complacency. Nevertheless, investors appear remarkably relaxed, and happy to add to their risk exposure. There’s plenty of optimism over tariffs, and a belief that trade deals will be agreed before next week’s deadline," David Morrison of Trade Nation said.

"As earnings season has heated up, we’re finally seeing who is swimming naked after the post-April rally seemed to have lifted all boats.  For now, the picture isn’t half bad, as the AI thesis remains strong, economic growth remains solid enough to avoid a recession, and secular growth trends like aerospace and economic re-shoring continue to flourish,” SA analyst Leo Nelissen stated.

Earnings continue to heat up as well with Tesla (TSLA) ending down 8% after CEO Elon Musk said the company could face a "few rough quarters." The firm avoided a second consecutive top- and bottom-line miss, with profits in line with expectations. Also, Alphabet (GOOGL)(GOOG) finished up 0.9% as the firm beat the expectations easily with another double-digit gain for revenues. 

“We would be buyers on any Tesla weakness. This is an AI story and the autonomous and robotics future is the future. Deliveries under near term pressure…but Musk is driving AI future and autonomous worth $1 trillion to the story. That’s our bull thesis and focus for Tesla,” long time tech bull Dan Ives said. 

“Alphabet’s quarter/comments should alleviate investor caution around the perceived risks of generative AI on Search biz. These concerns are overdone with Alphabet validating its ability to navigate this period of transition. Doubling down on AI Capex is bullish, $225 PT,” Ives added. 

Intel (INTC) is scheduled to report its latest quarterly results after the market closes, with investors closely watching for updates on the company’s turnaround efforts and performance in key segments.

data.title: CNX Resources guides for higher full-year production while capex drops
data.description:
CNX Resources (NYSE:CNX) -2.4% in Thursday's trading despite posting better than expected Q2 adjusted earnings and revenues, and raising guidance for full-year natural gas production while planning for capital spending to remain steady.

CNX's (NYSE:CNX) results follow reports earlier this week from two other major U.S. gas producers, EQT and Range Resources, which said in their earnings reports that they also would produce more energy this year than previously expected.

CNX (CNX) said it planned FY 2025 capital spending of $450M-$500M, the same as its Q1 guidance, to produce 1.68B-1.7B cfe/day, compared with its previous outlook for 1.66B-1.7B cfe/day; in FY 2024, the company spent ~$540M in capital and produced 1.51B cfe/day.

data.title: IMAX targets 150–160 system installations in 2025 amid record box office and expanding global footprint
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: IMAX Corporation (IMAX) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Richard L. Gelfond, CEO, reported that IMAX delivered "strong financial results in Q2, highlighted by installation growth of 50%, box office growth of over 40% and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 43%." He noted that 57 new and upgraded IMAX locations have opened year-to-date, compared to 39 during the same period last year. The company has now completed agreements for 124 new and upgraded IMAX systems year-to-date, nearing the 130 signed in all of 2024. Gelfond stated, "Q2 was our highest grossing quarter ever at the domestic box office as we remain on track to achieve our guidance of $1.2 billion for the full year."
  • Gelfond emphasized market share gains, stating, "Our global market share, which at 3.6% of total box office on less than 1% of screens is up 19% year-over-year in the second quarter." He attributed this to the “film for IMAX strategy” and highlighted that for major summer releases, IMAX averaged "about 15% of the North American box office on opening weekend on just 400 IMAX screens, soaring as high as 20% on Mission Impossible - The Final Reckoning, Sinners and F1."
  • Strategic expansion was highlighted, including IMAX's "largest single year expansion ever in France," network growth of "over 20% in Japan from last year," and a new agreement with Regal for its "first new location in Manhattan in 15 years."
  • Gelfond detailed partnerships: "Our recent agreement with Wanda will see IMAX systems replace existing premium format auditoriums in up to 27 locations, a sign of our dominant competitive position in China." He also mentioned ongoing partnerships in Australia and collaborations with Apple, Amazon, and Netflix for future film releases.
  • Natasha Fernandes, CFO, stated, "IMAX delivered another quarter of record-breaking results, driven by a 41% year-over-year increase in global box office, strong installation growth of 50% and an adjusted EBITDA margin exceeding 42% for the second straight quarter."

Outlook

  • Gelfond shared that, "Given the demand for IMAX systems, we're moving higher in our range for full year installations to between 150 and 160 systems worldwide."
  • Fernandes added, "Our results through the first half place us on track to meet or beat guidance for the full year, including on box office, system installations now expected to be between 150 and 160 for the year and adjusted EBITDA margin now expected to be in the low 40s."
  • Management noted confidence in future slate and backlog: "Looking beyond 2025, there is good visibility into IMAX's future installations as we have a significant and replenishing backlog."

Financial Results

  • Fernandes reported, "We delivered revenues of $92 million compared to $89 million in the prior year second quarter and achieved gross margin in Q2 of $54 million, which grew 22% year-over-year."
  • Content Solutions revenues were $34 million; gross margin was $22 million at a 66% margin. Technology Products and Services revenues reached $56 million, with a gross margin of $30 million at a 54% margin.
  • Installations for the quarter totaled 36 systems versus 24 in the prior year. The company now reports a backlog of 131 systems in the U.S., up 46% year-over-year.
  • Operating expenditures, excluding stock-based compensation, were $30 million in the quarter, down $3 million year-over-year. Restructuring costs year-to-date were over $840,000.
  • Consolidated adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was $39 million, rising $8 million year-over-year. Adjusted EPS was $0.26, up $0.08 from the previous year, with cash flow from operations at just over $30 million through the first half.
  • Cash at $109 million, debt excluding deferred financing costs at $280 million, and available liquidity at approximately $490 million.

Q&A

  • Omar Mejias Santiago, Wells Fargo, asked about the evolution of "film for IMAX" releases. CEO Gelfond responded that while the company will "lean into" such films for the right content, "we won't make it ubiquitous."
  • Santiago also asked about joint PLF marketing by U.S. theater chains. Gelfond stated, "A lot of people have tried to create competitors to IMAX over the years, but the fact is that our brand and relationships with filmmakers are unmatched and our technology is superior and audiences know."
  • Chad Beynon, Macquarie, inquired about pricing power. Gelfond said, "It definitely gives IMAX a stronger hand in our negotiations with content providers... but I think we're going to use that carefully."
  • Eric Handler, ROTH Capital, asked about product mix and alternative content. Gelfond replied, "Our percentage of local language content is around 40%, which is much higher than historically."
  • Eric Wold, Texas Capital, raised the topic of midweek ticket discounting. Gelfond responded, "I don't think the price we charge is keeping people away. I think people recognize it's a premium experience and they're willing to pay for it."
  • Michael Hickey, The Benchmark Company, asked about installation momentum. Gelfond replied, "We've almost equaled all the signings we had for the whole year last year... the backlog is being replenished with the new theaters coming online."
  • Natasha Fernandes addressed cash flow conversion, indicating operating cash flow was $30 million in the first half, up 25% year-over-year, and free cash flow is trending toward historical levels of around 50%.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on sustainability of growth, competitive positioning, pricing strategy, and cash flow. The tone was generally positive with probing for long-term sustainability and margin drivers.
  • Management’s tone was highly confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing phrases like "we have good reason to believe it will only get better" (Gelfond) and "we believe we're not just outperforming the market, we're expanding it" (Fernandes). In Q&A, management maintained confidence but was careful not to overpromise, especially regarding content strategy and pricing.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management sustained a similarly strong positive tone, with increased emphasis on momentum, backlog, and execution.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for system installations was raised from "145 to 160" in Q1 to "150 to 160" in Q2, reflecting higher confidence and demand.
  • Revenues increased from $87 million in Q1 to $92 million in Q2. Adjusted EPS rose from $0.13 in Q1 to $0.26 in Q2.
  • Operating expenditures decreased by $3 million year-over-year in Q2, versus a $950,000 increase in Q1.
  • Management’s confidence was elevated with more concrete global expansion milestones, new partnerships, and a replenishing backlog. Analyst focus shifted slightly from China and local language growth in Q1 to broader global network expansion and competitive positioning in Q2.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management referenced ongoing operational efficiency initiatives, including restructuring costs and the centralization of select functions.
  • No material new risks were highlighted; competition from other PLF offerings was addressed directly, with Gelfond dismissing them as "pathetic attempts to try and take a stand that is highly unlikely to work."
  • Analyst questions probed for risk around competitive threats, pricing models, and sustainability of growth, but management responses emphasized IMAX’s strong brand, backlog, and market share.

Final Takeaway

IMAX management highlighted a record-breaking quarter with robust installation growth, significant box office gains, and expanding global partnerships. The company raised its full-year system installation guidance and emphasized the strength of its backlog and upcoming film slate, supporting ongoing margin and cash flow growth. Management underscored the scalability of its model, global market share leadership, and strong capital position as key drivers for continued shareholder value in 2025 and beyond.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Veris Residential raises 2025 Core FFO guidance to $0.63–$0.64 amid accelerated asset sales and debt reduction focus
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Veris Residential, Inc. (VRE) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Mahbod Nia highlighted, “The second quarter marked another period of solid operational and financial results for Veris, including $0.17 of Core FFO and 5.6% Same Store NOI growth.” He noted that $450 million of nonstrategic asset sales have either been completed or are under contract, nearly fulfilling the company’s $300 million to $500 million asset sale target well ahead of schedule. Nia emphasized that proceeds will be used “primarily to reduce leverage to around 10x by year-end 2025 and are on track to further reduce this to below 9x by year-end 2026.”
  • Nia reported a 55 basis point improvement in borrowing costs due to the amended credit facility and mentioned significant recent sales, including Signature Place and 145 Front Street, sold for $85 million and $122 million, respectively. He indicated the company is “well positioned to sustain strong rental growth going forward.”
  • COO Anna Malhari stated, “Our portfolio achieved a blended net rental growth rate of 4.7% for the quarter, up from 2.3% in the first quarter and well above the national average of 1%.” She detailed technology-driven operational improvements, such as proprietary revenue management tools and AI-powered leasing solutions, resulting in annualized cost savings.
  • CFO Amanda Lombard reported, “For the second quarter of 2025, net income available to common shareholders was $0.12 per fully diluted share versus $0.03 for the prior year and a net loss of $0.12 in the first quarter.” She added, “Core FFO per share was $0.17 for the second quarter, up $0.01 from the first quarter due to several factors, including the transactions closed in the period.”

Outlook

  • Lombard announced, “We are raising our Core FFO guidance range to $0.63 to $0.64 from $0.61 to $0.63 per share.” This reflects year-to-date portfolio performance, completed and pending asset sales, and a reduction in debt. She projected Core FFO growth of 5% to 6.7% compared to 2024.
  • Same Store NOI guidance was also increased to a range of 2% to 2.8% owing to robust leasing spreads, recent sales, and realized operational savings.
  • The company anticipates G&A will be flat year-over-year, with a third-quarter uptick expected for seasonal activities.

Financial Results

  • Core FFO per share rose to $0.17 for the quarter, with $0.01 attributed to nonrecurring income and property management expense savings.
  • Same Store NOI growth was 5.6% in Q2, and year-to-date growth stands at 4.4%.
  • Same Store rental revenue increased by 2.5% for the quarter. Excluding Liberty Towers and other one-time income, rental revenue growth would have been 3.8%.
  • Controllable expenses were down 3.7% year-over-year, aided by reduced marketing and administrative costs and lower repairs at Liberty Towers. Non-controllable expenses, including insurance and real estate tax, decreased 3.2% year-over-year.
  • Net debt-to-EBITDA was 11.3x (trailing 12 months), not reflecting asset sales closed and under contract after June 30. Liquidity increased to $181 million following the repayment of a $200 million term loan.

Q&A

  • Stephen Thomas Sakwa, Evercore ISI: Asked about Board and CIO changes, and impact on asset dispositions. Nia responded, “We have a team, an investment team headed by Brian Primost … well positioned to continue executing on our plan.” On Board changes, he said Ron’s departure was due to “fiduciary obligations,” and “Ron continues to be highly supportive of us and the company's strategy.”
  • Sakwa questioned guidance assumptions for taxes and insurance. Lombard explained, “We did assume that we would have a mid- to high single-digits renewal. … real estate taxes in Jersey City have been volatile in the prior years.”
  • Sakwa inquired about future asset sales. Nia acknowledged, “It's an extremely challenging transaction market and difficult to predict,” but emphasized ongoing efforts to realize NAV for smaller assets.
  • Eric Jon Wolfe, Citi: Asked if the market for larger buildings is weaker. Nia replied, “There's definitely a discount for size today,” and noted most remaining assets are smaller and more marketable.
  • Wolfe also probed challenges in recent transactions. Nia shared, “Relative to replacement cost, I would say those represented something of a discount, not a huge discount, but something of a discount to replacement cost.”
  • Anthony Paolone, JPMorgan: Queried about addressing next year’s debt maturities. Lombard replied, “We believe we'll have the capacity to repay off all of those through those needs.” Nia added, “The asset quality that we have, the markets that we're in … put us in really good stead to be able to manage through those refinancings.”
  • Paolone asked about capital allocation priorities. Nia stated, “Leverage reduction is the priority.”
  • Jana Galan, Bank of America: Asked about potential impact of New York City policy changes. Nia said, “It's a little bit too soon to draw any definitive conclusions,” but recognized possible benefits for Jersey City if taxes rise in New York.
  • Galan requested July blended rent spreads. Lombard indicated, “We still continue to send out renewals around the mid-single digits.”
  • John Joseph Pawlowski, Green Street: Followed up on land sales prospects. Nia noted, “It's certainly a difficult transaction market for land … we would like to make some more progress.”
  • Pawlowski asked about Liberty Towers occupancy trajectory. Lombard replied, “There may be some volatility, but we would hope to be in the low 80s going forward.” Full renovations are expected to take about three years.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a generally neutral to slightly positive tone, pressing for details on asset disposition challenges, debt management, and operational resilience. Recent management changes and transaction market difficulties were probed without overt skepticism.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing progress “well ahead of schedule” and “sustained momentum,” but acknowledged transaction complexities and market headwinds. During Q&A, management demonstrated measured confidence, with Nia often highlighting the team’s adaptability and stating, “I think we've created many options for ourselves going forward.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts exhibited increased cautious optimism, with management now more explicit in outlining deleveraging progress and operational improvements, and analysts focusing more on execution risks and near-term challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Core FFO guidance was raised this quarter, whereas it was reaffirmed last quarter despite positive developments, reflecting greater confidence in operational and transactional progress.
  • The company accelerated its nonstrategic asset sales, nearing the upper end of its target range ahead of schedule, compared to a slower pace last quarter.
  • Management’s tone shifted from caution—due to macroeconomic and policy uncertainties—to more confident articulation of strategic execution and improved financial metrics.
  • Analysts this quarter focused more on the sustainability of asset sales, refinancing, and the impact of management changes, while previously emphasizing transaction market uncertainty and guidance conservatism.
  • Key metrics such as rental growth and NOI growth improved sequentially, with blended net rental growth rising from 2.4% in Q1 to 4.7% in Q2, and Same Store NOI growth accelerating from 3.2% to 5.6%.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing challenges in the transaction market, particularly for larger assets and land, requiring a pragmatic approach and measured expectations for future sales.
  • Volatility in Jersey City real estate taxes and insurance premiums remains a risk, with Lombard noting their potential material impacts on guidance.
  • Execution risks associated with asset sales chains and buyer financing were referenced as possible headwinds to further deleveraging.
  • Analysts expressed concern about the sustainability of current momentum in dispositions and the ability to manage refinancing needs for 2026 maturities, which management addressed by outlining liquidity and asset quality advantages.

Final Takeaway

Veris Residential’s second quarter results underscore accelerated execution of its deleveraging strategy, robust rental growth well above national averages, and tangible cost savings from technology investments. Management’s increased confidence is reflected in raised Core FFO and NOI guidance, supported by significant asset sales and improved balance sheet flexibility. While the company faces ongoing transaction market and tax volatility risks, its proactive balance sheet management and operational outperformance position it to continue advancing strategic goals into the second half of 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Restaurant check: Sweetgreen, CAVA, and Wingstop follow Chipotle lower
data.description:
The restaurant sector was jolted on Thursday as investors sized up Chipotle's (NYSE:CMG) soft Q2 earnings report.

The fast-casual chain reported same-store sales fell 4%, driven mainly by a 4.9% drop in transactions. Revenue still edged up 3% year-over-year to $3.1 billion, aided primarily by new restaurant openings. Restaurant-level margin slipped to 27.4%, down 150 basis points from the prior year. Despite sequential improvement in June and July due to stronger marketing, ongoing volatility led Chipotle (NYSE:CMG) to lower its full-year guidance to an outlook for flat comparable sales.

During the earnings conference call, Chipotle (CMG) Chief Executive Scott Boatwright pointed to ongoing volatility and the consumer environment as headwinds.

Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG) fell 11.8% in Thursday afternoon trading to more than erase a strong gain on Wednesday when traders were snapping up names with a high level of short interest outstanding on them.

Elsewhere within the restaurant sector, Portillo's (PTLO) fell 7.4%, First Watch Restaurant Group (FWRG) slid 6.1%, CAVA Group (NYSE:CAVA) peeled off 5.6%, and Wingstop (NASDAQ:WING) declined 5.5% as investors sized up the Chipotle (CMG) update.
